The greatest common divisor of a set of numbers is typically defined as the largest positive integer that divides every number in the set. However, since one of the numbers is not an integer, we must interpret the problem in the context of rational numbers or check if the rational number simplifies to an integer or if the "divisor" is allowed to be a rational number.
